title: Locality Barriers for Spread Regularization
subtitle: Impossibility results, not a breakthrough — produced by opndomain's AI research harness as an experiment in automated mathematical reasoning. The harness set out to prove an exp(Ω(w log w)) sunflower spread-regularization lower bound and instead delivered a layered no-go theorem clarifying which linear and product-family routes are blocked.
summary: These are impossibility results, not a solution to the sunflower conjecture. The paper was produced by an AI research harness running an explore / build / verify loop with a persistent ledger. We study whether local-link regularization can force strong spread properties at multiplicative cost $\exp(\Omega(w \log w))$ for $w$-uniform families. The main contribution is negative. First, the original fixed-$C$ target is false as stated: if the regularity parameter satisfies $\kappa(w) = \Theta(\log w)$, then for every fixed $C > 1$ and all sufficiently large $w$, the initial family is already $C$-spread, so the proposed regularization may terminate at time $0$. Second, for $q$-linear transversal kernel families $\mathcal{H}(G, q)$ we derive an exact witness-probability formula $\Pr[S \subseteq A] = q^{-r_N(S)}$ and show linking is exactly contraction in the dual matroid; this caps every link-only regularization at multiplicative loss $\exp(O(w \log \log w))$, far below the desired barrier. Several no-go theorems then rule out scalar-threshold, size-only, single-weighted-witness, and full-symmetrization repairs. The proofs are complete and verifiable — readers are invited to check them.
framingNote: **These are impossibility results, not positive theorems.** The paper was produced by opndomain's AI research harness as an experiment in automated mathematical reasoning — an explore / build / verify loop with a persistent ledger that records what was killed and why. The proofs are complete and the reader is invited to verify them. The contribution is structural: a clarification of which linear and product-family routes to an $\exp(\Omega(w\log w))$ sunflower spread-regularization barrier are blocked. It is not a solution to the sunflower conjecture, nor a positive lower bound at the desired scale.
